scope_boundary: synthetic tribunal-review record discipline and finite Lean tribunal predicates only; no reviewer independence, adversarial-review quality, consensus quality, verdict correctness, source interpretation, runtime behavior, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository now has a tribunal-review schema, a protocol fixture, finite Tribunal Lean predicates, proof adequacy review text that keeps tribunal formalization narrow, exact source mappings for the UAT chapter, and a synthetic tribunal review harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures. Those artifacts make dossier refs, reviewer roles, adversarial probes, evidence-backed accept verdicts, dissent preservation, prior-review guards, required actions, constraint effects, and non-claim boundaries executable at the record level. They still do not establish reviewer independence, adversarial-review quality, consensus quality, verdict correctness, source interpretation adequacy, or a deployed tribunal pipeline.
required_artifacts: multi-reviewer tribunal run over real claims or artifacts with preserved dossiers and reviewer outputs
reviewer-independence audit or role-separation enforcement trace
adversarial-probe quality review with positive and negative controls
verdict correctness or adjudication-quality review against known outcomes
dissent preservation test across actual accepted and rejected reviews
prior-review replay showing unchanged-evidence laundering is blocked on real review records
independent review of source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_tribunal_review.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py && python3 scripts/validate_source_evidence_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: Unified Adaptive Tribunal remains at argument
schema validation checks record shape rather than tribunal behavior
finite Lean predicates remain useful but too narrow for tribunal-quality claims
reviewer independence, adversarial-probe quality, consensus quality, verdict correctness, source interpretation review, and deployed tribunal traces are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: chapter text interpreted as reviewer-independence evidence
synthetic adversarial-probe fixture interpreted as adversarial-review quality evidence
accept verdict fixture interpreted as verdict correctness evidence
prior-review guard fixture treated as deployed anti-laundering behavior
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic record discipline that passed, or after real tribunal runs, reviewer-independence review, adversarial-probe quality review, verdict-correctness review, dissent-preservation tests, prior-review replay, and independent source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not run multiple independent reviewers
schema validation does not prove adversarial review quality or consensus quality
finite Lean predicates do not implement a tribunal pipeline
accept-with-scope fixtures do not prove verdict correctness
prior-review refs in fixtures do not prove a deployed anti-laundering replay guard
no independent reviewer-quality or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent tribunal-design, reviewer-quality, adjudication-quality, formal-methods, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no dissent-preservation test over real reviews
no prior-review replay over real records
non_claims: does not promote Unified Adaptive Tribunal above argument
does not prove consensus quality or verdict correctness
does not prove runtime behavior or deployed tribunal operation
